dominant_bottleneck.statement: Orchestration gaps across engines lead to contract drift and missing readiness signals.

highest_risk_trust_gap.statement: Docs-to-runtime drift can make trust assumptions bypassable when replay/control invariants are not uniformly tested.

A_foundation: Authority parity checks are not yet formalized as reusable compiler contract
Roadmap artifact schemas are not yet pinned for this slice
B_governed_operation: BN-006 orchestration governance remains partial across surfaces
C_learning: Eval assets are uneven across systems; learning loops are partial
D_control: Control coverage exceeds explicit invariant tests, creating bypass risk
E_application: System-specific executable adapters lag architecture coverage
F_hardening: Certification gate is partial and dead-surface candidates remain
G_constitutional_alignment: Continuous conformance checks to canonical runtime rules are not yet enforced in this slice

top_risks: BN-006 orchestration drift risk across engines
Partial eval/certification leaves control surfaces incompletely hardened
Potential docs-to-runtime drift without stronger invariant tests
